items.title: Convicted rapist back in custody after Sydney hospital escape
items.standfirst: A man who escaped immigration detention while waiting to be deported has been arrested after five days on the run.
items.content: A man who escaped immigration detention while waiting to be deported has been arrested after five days on the run.

items.title: Gang rapist detained after five-day manhunt in Sydney
items.standfirst: The manhunt for a convicted gang rapist, who managed to escape immigration detention is over.
items.content: The manhunt for a convicted gang rapist who allegedly escaped immigration detention is over.Michael Angok was arrested at Seven Hills in Sydney's west this afternoon.He had 100 hours of freedom before being arrested about a 40-minute drive from where he escaped in Bankstown.READ MORE: Injured driver charged over alleged road rage attack on motorbike ridersHe's spent the last nine years in Villawood Detention Centre, but on Wednesday morning allegedly escaped from Bankstown Hospital while receiving treatment.The 30-year-old was jailed for the gang rape of a 14 year-old-girl in Doonside in 2014.He was awaiting deportation.Police arrested the man and took him to Blacktown police station, where he was charged on the outstanding warrant.He was refused bail to appear in Blacktown Local Court tomorrow.Investigations are continuing.

items.title: Police charge gang rapist who escaped from Sydney hospital five days ago
items.standfirst: On Wednesday, Michael Angok fled on foot from Bankstown Hospital shortly after 9:30am. He had been taken there from Villawood Detention Centre.
items.content: On Wednesday, Michael Angok fled on foot from Bankstown Hospital shortly after 9:30am. He had been taken there from Villawood Detention Centre.

clusters.title: Tradie assaulted in road rage fight
clusters.neutral_headline: Tradie assaulted in road rage fight

items.title: Tradie assaulted in road rage fight
items.standfirst: A young tradie has been brutally assaulted after an argument with another driver escalated in Melbourne.
items.content: A young tradie has been brutally assaulted after an argument with another driver escalated in Melbourne.

items.title: 'Felt all my teeth just crumble': Tradie assaulted in road rage fight
items.standfirst: A young tradie has recounted the moment he was brutally assaulted after an argument with another driver escalated.
items.content: Warning: This story contains images some readers may find distressing.A tradie has recounted the moment he was brutally assaulted after an argument with another driver escalated. The road rage attack in Melbourne left him bloodied with broken teeth. "I just went straight into fight or flight, you know, like protect myself. I thought I was done for, to be honest. I've never been in a situation like that before," Ciaran said.READ MORE: Two critical after five-car crash on Melbourne freeway The 21-year-old was struck in the head with a bat, kicked and punched in a vicious road rage attack. "They were screaming at me, 'we're gonna f---ing kill you', calling me a rat and stuff," he said."I don't even know these guys."He says the argument began when he beeped his horn at a BMW travelling at half the speed limit in Keilor Downs."They were pushing me off the road, my car was hitting the rims on the side," he said."Both vehicles collided when he tried to cross lanes and instead was pushed into a fence.READ MORE: Government faces backlash over $20m fuel campaign "And as I turned to get out of the car, I got a bat into the face and instantly I just felt all my teeth just crumble into my mouth."Blood instantly coming everywhere, I tried backing away from them."The driver and his passengers continued to lay into the apprentice plumber."All I was focused on is protecting my face 'cause it was bleeding, you know, so I'm protecting my face, trying not to let my teeth come out. So I'm just getting kicked in the back of the head," he said.LIVE UPDATES: 'Bad news': US fails to strike peace deal with Iran in Pakistan "I was pretty scared, but I did get up to my feet just out of pure adrenaline and run in front of traffic, in front of oncoming traffic to try and get away from them, which did work."The BMW fled before police arrived. They're now working to identify those men to hear their side of the story.Anyone who knows them is being urged to come forward.The victim also warned other drivers."Definitely just be careful who you beep at, I guess, you know, sometimes it's not worth it," he said.

clusters.title: Police probe if ‘kill car’ was being driven by kill crew for hire
clusters.neutral_headline: Police probe if ‘kill car’ was being driven by kill crew for hire
bullet_summary: More details have been revealed about three young men caught driving in an alleged "kill car" rammed after a high-speed chase in Sydney
Detectives are investigating if the crew was planning to carry out an underworld hit with the pistol and rifle allegedly found in the stolen Audi

items.title: Police probe if ‘kill car’ was being driven by kill crew for hire
items.standfirst: More details have been revealed about three young men caught driving in an alleged "kill car" rammed after a high-speed chase in Sydney.
items.content: More details have been revealed about three young men caught driving in an alleged "kill car" rammed after a high-speed chase in Sydney.

items.title: Police probe if 'kill car' was being driven by crime crew for hire
items.standfirst: Detectives are investigating if the crew was planning to carry out an underworld hit with the pistol and rifle allegedly found in the stolen Audi.
items.content: More details have been revealed about three young men caught driving in an alleged "kill car" that was rammed after a high-speed chase in Sydney.Detectives are investigating whether the crew was hired to carry out an underworld hit outside the Wenty Leagues club in Wentworthville.Police rammed the allegedly stolen Audi after chasing it from Wentworthville to Lidcombe on the Great Western Highway.READ MORE: Two homes sprayed with bullets in Sydney's west They arrested three men, including 28-year-old Dean Saunders and 24-year-old Chayce Jensen and charged them with weapons offences and participating in a criminal group.Raptor squad police began hunting the trio after they were allegedly spotted wearing balaclavas inside the white Audi in the carpark of Wenty Leagues about 2pm yesterday.Detectives are investigating if the crew was planning to carry out an underworld hit with the pistol and rifle allegedly found in the stolen Audi.The car is linked to several underworld shooting and fire bombings across south-west Sydney.That includes a shooting on March 14, a firebombing on March 20 and another drive-by last Tuesday on the same Panania house.On the same night police alleged the stolen car was used in shootings at Colyton and St John's Park.9News is not suggesting those arrested are involved in these other crimes. Police say the target who lives at the house in Panania is linked to the G7 gang.Members of this gang are often young men who are hired by heavyweight criminals to carry out underworld contracts.The three accused didn't apply for bail in court today but plan to in coming weeks.9News understands they don't have any strong links to any criminal groups and police allege they are a crime crew for hire.The Audi is being forensically examined and investigators say it could have been used by a number of different criminals for dozens of underworld jobs.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here
